text: We got this TV in Nov 2005 from BB . It worked great until the one year mark . It started turn itself off after an hour . We could not turn it back on for at least 5 minutes . We called Samsung , and they sent a repairman out to fix the problem . That was November 17 , 2006 . Less than 3 months later , we are having the SAME problem . Only , we have to pay out of pocket for repairs because they said we are out of warrantee . Samsung 's service department quoted me around $ 500 for repairs that Samsung ca n't figure out what is causing the problem . We paid $ 800 for the tv and the TV is barely a year old . Ridiculous ! The service center suggested I contact Samsung 's customer service to get an extended warrantee since the TV is so new still . So I spoke to Amanda at Samsung customer service and then her supervisor , Mike # 43 . Neither were very helpful nor sympathetic towards my issue . Mike had the `` I 'm wasting his time '' attitude and told me to call the Extended Warrantee department to see if they can do anything . If not , then call the local tv repair person . So I called the Extended Warrantee department and was told that only the customer service department can handle extended warrantees ! I called back to the Samsung customer service department and thankfully spoke to someone who might be able to help me . I just need to fax in my bill of sale when I get home tonight and hopefully someone at Samsung can send out a repair person to fix the TV without us having to pay the price of another tv for repairs . Two repairs and the TV is barely a year old . Do n't waste your time and money on this TV . Get a Sony instead . This TV should be recalled .

text: - Durability : 10\/5 When I say 10\/5 I mean it . 3 days after X-mas , I dropped my brand new iPod 4G on a stone-tile floor -LRB- about 3-4 feet up -RRB- and it shattered -LRB- Apple replaced it free of charge : D -RRB- , yet I 'd still give the iPod 4G a 4.5 \/ 5 in durability . I 've dropped my iPad 2 on the same floor maybe 2x . It got a tiny dent in the aluminum frame . I have dropped this iPad on wood floors , ceramic tile , stone tile , concrete , asphalt , you name it -LRB- yes , grass , too -RRB- . I do not , nor have I ever , had a case on it . Sure , the edges of the case are dented beyond repair , but the screen and -LRB- surprisingly -RRB- the back of the frame are scuff-free . BUT !!! However you drop it , DO NOT drop anything on it ! My TV remote slipped from my hand less than 1 foot above my screen , and it obliterated the home button , which I now keep in my pocket . I found it extremely ironic : -RRB- The screen is impossible to scratch , however . Not gouge , but scratch . Since it 's broken , I ordered a new screen . I decided to see how tough the screens are by scratching one with a nail -LRB- as in hammer and nail , not finger nail -RRB- . It could not scratch it . Since I do n't remember much of the Mohs Hardness scale , and I lack a diamond , it 's somewhere between a 3 and 10 hardness . - Personalization .1 \/ 5 Apple has too much restricted customer customization of this product . As soon as you get this product , JAILBREAK IT ! Jailbreaks are not illegal unless you install illegal sources . If you do n't like any aspect of Apple 's software designs , from there being a six-icon limit on the dock , to being able to run all your applications in windows or have Siri on the iPad , Jailbreak it . 99.9382 % of the time you 'll find the tweak you 're looking for . - Size 2.3 \/ 5 Frankly , 2 years ago when I was 13 I was flipping out to get such a big electronic toy . Now I think it 's too freaking big . If you want a size that is good for presentations , yet still retaining good mobility , go with an iPad . If you want something you can stick in you 're jacket pocket yet still be able to read , get something nearby the size of a Kindle Fire , even though I personally think iPad is 700x better . - Battery Life 5\/5 ... I sometimes sit there and watch the battery drain , because I 'm afraid if I open an app it will die -LRB- obviously this is at maybe 5-30 % battery life -RRB- . Yet sometimes I 've found I can play for 4-5 hours , on a 3D game , no less , from 100 % to dead . - Display ... The part we 've all been waiting for ... Honestly , I 'd have to say ... 4.6 \/ 5 I thought the display was absolutely stunning . That is , until I saw the iPad 3 . I started seeing minor -LRB- MINOR !!!!!! -RRB- flaws on edges . This was mostly in a few particular apps designed for both iPhone and iPad , most of the other apps were just fine . If you do n't mind not having 3,100,000 pixels , take 786,432 pixels instead . You wo n't notice a difference , so long as you avoid iPad 3 's . - Versatility 5\/5 I use this for school , homework , games , browsing the web to buy a new screen , FaceTiming with friends , staying up-to-date with friends , you name it . It even makes a good paperweight : -RRB- - Overall 4.85 \/ 5 Very satisfied .

text: My Nook Tablet and I are inseparable ! Everything that entertains and informs me all in my coat pocket . If it 's comes down to whether you should buy a Kindle Fire or a Nook Tablet , Nook clearly wins . Yes , it 's 50 bucks more but it 's 50 dollars well spent . The Nook has more memory and a faster processor than the Fire . In real end-user terms that means you get the responsive light touch and quick displays iPad users know so well . Kindle Fire does n't quite cut it . Most important -LRB- and I do n't know why this is n't a no-brainer -RRB- you can store all your pics , docs , music and books on the Nook itself . The Fire requires a constant connection to the Internet . If you travel around enough or ride commuter trains , you know you 're not always going to find wi-fi everywhere you go . A fat lot of good having my books out on the `` cloud '' when there 's no wi-fi around . But here 's the best part of all . You can plug in Micro-SD card that will boot up the Nook into a full Android tablet . That gives you access to the thousands of apps on the Android Market -- including Amazon 's Kindle app . Now you have all the advantages of the par $ 500 -- $ 600 range tablets at half the price ! Do n't worry about voiding your warranty . Just take the SD card out and it reverts back instantly to the Nook Tablet you bought .

text: I 've owned a Tivoli Model 1 and 3 , a Boston Acoustics Receptor , an Eton 100 , and the Sangean WR-2 , which gets the gold medal among an admittedly highly competitive field . Its FM sensitivity and selectivity are terrific in my area -LRB- midway between Chicago and Milwaukee -RRB- , and the radio scanned and locked into the good stations automatically within seconds after plugging it in . The sound is absolutely transparent and open , with no hint of boosted frequencies or `` canned '' dispersion . The main `` downside '' of the radio is the diminutive , BB-like buttons employed for setting stations and the alarm . Be prepared to spend some time learning to read the buttons like braille -LRB- the print is tiny and barely accessible to most sight-lines -RRB- and to use the remote as a quicker and easier way to control certain features of the radio even when it 's right next to your bedside . What draws me to this model is its versatility . It has three auxiliary jacks -LRB- headphones on front ; playback and recording inputs on back -RRB- , effective and user-friendly treble and bass tone controls , and offers a generous and flat upper surface area for a CD player or iPod -LRB- a dome-like speaker bulges from the top of the Tivoli 3 , and the Receptor has a slanting and slippery top slope -RRB- . Moreover , like the Receptor , the Sangean requires no power adapter , or `` wall wart '' -LRB- the Tivoli comes with a small one ; the Eton with a relatively large one -RRB- , yet the W-2 has room-filling power , with a volume control that 's smooth and `` infinite '' in its gradations -LRB- settings from 1 to 60 ! -RRB- In sum , the sound is about equal to the Tivoli -LRB- though the latter has slightly tighter , better defined bass and , to my ears , a tad more mid-range `` presence '' -RRB- , and if you want some of the bass heaviness of the Receptor or Eton , the tone controls allow for that possibility . It lacks the simple non-digital tuning of the Tivoli , but the trade-off is knowing at all times what station you 're locked into . The alarm system is more complicated than the others upon initial set-up , but with practice and patience -LRB- there is no `` dedicated '' snooze bar -RRB- it 's feasible -- especially if you do n't change settings from day to day . The walnut finish is close to the look of bare , unfinished wood ; consequently , black may be a safer bet in some decors . The display is large and the RDS especially effective on this machine -LRB- though unfortunately the time does not show in RDS mode -RRB- . The radio weighs a bit more and is a little wider than the other three . -LRB- The overachieving Receptor can practically be thrown into a suitcase for traveling yet delivers bass frequencies that are impressively or excessively floor-shaking , depending on your point of view . -RRB- I rate the Tivoli , Receptor and this Sangean as 5-star radios , so personal preference rules . Primarily because of the digital display , headphone jack , and CD input , the Sangean comes closest to the clock radio I would have designed , though the alarm set may understandably frustrate traditionalists and those devoted to the KISS principle . -LRB- Warning : the instruction manual is often closer to pidgin than English . You might do better to learn Spanish and read that part of the manual instead . -RRB-

text:
This phone does so many things so well I hate to criticize it 's few faults .

The good .

1 . Backlit display and keyboard .

2 . Easy to add numbers to memory .

3 . Easy to edit numbers in memory .

4 . Multiple handsets can be in use at a time .

5 . Global or specific phone page . Intercom between phones .

6 . Nice loud ringtones ... many different sounds .

7 . Speakerphone is loud and clear ... one of the best I 've heard on a consumer product .

8 . Good range and sound quality .

The not so good .

1 . Manual a bit confusing and slim .

2 . Menus can be counterintuitive .

3 . Dialing numbers from caller I.D. easier than from memory -LRB- so why use memory ??? -RRB-

4 . Must retrieve messages from answering machine ... ca n't get from handsets . Of course you can get your messages from another phone by inputting codes .

5 . Ca n't transfer phone list from phone to phone .

6 . Slightly echoy sounding ... but not distracting .

7 . No speakerphone in base unit . Vtech sells another version with this feature . I wish they had added it to this `` higher level version . ''

Overall , despite the shortcomings , this is the best multi-handset system out there . The speakerphone works so well that I ca n't imagine buying a phone without this feature . I can be in the kitchen , cooking , handsfree , while having a discussion with my spouse on another floor .

I want to give it 5 stars because it does so much but there is room for improvement -LRB- are you listening Vtech ? -RRB-

text: While I start by stating my review will not be as long as other who have analyzed every nuiance of these phones , I will state what I have observed . 1 . I prefer over the ear versus all others . 2 . There is an adjustment period , as with any new phones to come to grips with . My previous set was JBL , which I still believe were the best I have ever owned . What I did not like about them was point # 1 , there were not over the ear , only on the ear , but the sound , like the speakers was magnificent and clear . So , no matter what phones you currently own , there will be an adjustment to the sound . 3 . The sound on these is clear , with the exception of the bass . Bass is highly distorted , especially the louder you crank up the volume . It is actually painful to the ear . These are the only phones , in 40 years , I have had that issue with ... these actually sound better on a softer volume . 4 . They are magnificient at blocking out ambient noise ! 5 . These are a great price for a pair of Sennheiser , especially if you do not want to spend $ 300 + for their higher models , or spend less . Again , the one draw for me was the over the ear factor . 6 . Overall , these are a very good set of phones , at a decent price ... you will not be disappointed , with the exception of some bass distortion , especially on punk or dance music .

text: I feel a bit of a fool , because how do you get this thing stuck in your ear ? But it happened to me , and it was n't as if I did anything extraordinarily stupid . I read the instruction booklet , and I stuck the earphones in my ears . That 's all I basically did . I attached one of the smaller cushiony sleeves to each side of the earphones . They were really comfy -- slid right into my ear , felt just right -- like they were positioned the way they were supposed to be . Then one time when I was trying to remove the earphone from one side , the plastic sleeve detached from the earphone and stuck in my ear . I could n't get it out by myself , so off to the ER I went . The ER doctor said it got really stuck in there . I try to think back and wonder what I possibly did wrong that could have contributed to the ER visit , and I still come back to , `` I put them in my ears . I positioned them so they were comfortable and worked well . That 's all I did . '' Seriously , it is n't rocket science . The sleeves were attached snugly to the earphone body , and yet one of them still came off inside my ear . If it can happen to me , it can happen to someone else . Other than the trip to the ER , these earphones worked fine . The short time I had them , they really isolated outside sound well , and the sound was great . I wish they 'd work out for me , but understandably , I am leery of in-ear headphones right now -- at least ones with removable sleeves that \* might \* slip off inside your ear canal .

text: I like to use my Black & Decker FHV1200 Flex Vac Cordless Ultra-Compact Vacuum Cleaner -LRB- if I can be said to like to clean -RRB- . But there are definite pros and cons . There are things about it that I really like : It truly is compact , measuring about 12 x 6 x10 . And it 's light enough to handle easily . It 's cordless , which is a big advantage in cleaning hard to reach places . And the high-performance motor provides great suction . It comes with a 4-foot flexible hose , long crevice tool , nozzle\/brush tool , and pet-hair cleaning tool which increase its scope . And the filter is dishwasher-safe . The major downside is the performance time - about five minutes . It 's long enough to clean the car or swipe up kitty litter or even clean pet hair off the sofa , but it 's not long enough for real cleaning . Cleaning the dirt reservoir can be a bit dicey -LRB- but this to me is the major disadvantage of all bag-less vacuums -RRB- . And I 'm not a fan of the attachment storage caddy . So , if you have a ` real ' vacuum and are looking for a little guy for quick or hard to reach jobs , this may be your baby . Is it worth the $ $ $ $ ? It 's well made and works well , but it 's performance time is short .

text: Pros : - Battery Lasts a good amount of time - given the context of what it was intended for . text: I have owned many vacuums . I seem to be pretty tough on them , but then again ... if you have a family of eight with five out of those eight having waist-length hair , you do end up vacuuming quite a bit , generally every day . - I owned a Kirby ; it was awesome but the bags were so expensive and hard to get , and the attatchments were a pain to use . - I owned a Dyson Animal . UGH . Never again . I ca n't believe that piece of junk costs as much as it does . Horrible construction , the attatchments never worked properly , and bagless vacuums are just so disgusting . Not very many things will make me gag , but cleaning out a bagless vacuum does . Just ... no. . No. . Never . - I have owned several Dirt Devils . They are pretty good , but flimsy . But , they 're generally inexpensive , so the flimsiness is forgivable . -LRB- A Dirt Devil is what I 'm getting next after this one ... -RRB- - This vacuum is okay as far as suction , but it has so much suction that it becomes a liability . It literally walks across my carpet by itself on the highest settings , and it is all I can do to pull it back . However , you would think that amount of suction would leave your carpets really clean . Not so . With mine , it actually seemed to push everything down -LRB- instead of fluffing it up -RRB- and small crumbs would just fly everywhere vs. being picked up in the vacuum . I thought perhaps the lift mechanism was n't working ... that perhaps I had it set for high carpets but it actually was n't any higher than it would have been for low ones . But no. . . when I took it in on a flat surface I could definitely tell a difference in the high and low settings . It was just not high enough for my carpet , I guess . And this is n't any 1970 's shag carpet ! It 's just a plain pile carpet we bought from Lowes three years ago ! So ... I 'm going to keep this vacuum as a backup I guess . Since I 've used it three times , I 'm not sure if I can return it , and at worst I suppose we can use it in the garage\/shop for sawdust , etc. . As for what I 'm going to use in the house , I just ordered another Dirt Devil for $ 44 , and so what if I have to replace it every year ... at least it will clean my carpets and not pull my arm off in the process !
label: Vacuum

text: The vacuum is exactly as described , very quiet , excellent strong engine , does a super job without my having to lean way over . The posture and position of using this saves one from ergonomic disasters . Much less stress to the back and shoulders . A great bay , good price . Thanks ...
label: Vacuum

text: Purchased the Miele Twist after much research and consumer review . Love the machine . Let me say that I am a vacuum fanatic and use my vacuum at least every other day , and I mean at least -LRB- I have 3 kids , 2 dogs , and a dirty backyard -RRB- . Immediately the power of the vacuum was noted and even though it is a bit heavy on the pull-back , it is BECAUSE of the 2-motor design and so I will take power with weight any day . The carpet looks the best I have seen it in at least a couple of years -LRB- animal stains aside -RRB- and I think I am almost addicted to running it just to see the carpet plush , full and hear the dirt cyle through the hose -LRB- before the kids come home \* wink \* -RRB- . I worried about how it would handle my 15-staircase , but the hose extended to at least 12 stairs quite beautifully and it was not difficult to balance the vacuum while I cleaned the last upper three . Another thing I was curious about was the length of the cord , but again - I have been pleased to note that I can vacuum all of my downstairs from one convientently placed outlet . Speaking of downstairs , I adore how easy it is to shift from carpet to vinyl with just the flip of the switch and it does such a great job cleaning too . But , if you noted I gave it 4-stars vs 5 and you may be wondering why . Well , here are my quirks with the machine . While I love how long the cord is - I do not like how it can not seem to stay wound up on the machine , it falls off loop-by-loop as I move around the room . I plan to amend this annoyance by getting a velcro loop and making my own sort of contraption to keep it on the machine . I also find annoyance with a particular situation , that may be only related to me -LRB- and my own amazingly charged personality \* wink \* -RRB- , but it will shock me if the machine gets too close to my leg while running it . I learned with research that the machine uses an electrobrush -LRB- see -LRB- ... -RRB- -RRB- and this may be the cause of my experience . If I remember to keep it away and not pull it so close to my heart , as I want since I love it so , then it is fine . \* smile \* Now , on the principle of sandwiching , I must leave you with another loved aspect of this machine . I love the manuverability of the machine . It is like the Dyson ball , in effect , but the power and allergen control of a Miele . I can bring it low and it does n't alter how it works , and I can easily move it between and around my furniture . All in all - I am in love . The cord does n't stay on the machine very well and I get a small shock every now and then , but other than that - I adore the machine . For the price , it is a real bargain .
label: Vacuum

text: I use this everyday because I live in a dust\/desert area and have 2 big dogs . I have had this product for over a year and am very happy . The attachment for dog hair works great . This is not meant to clean as long as a regular vacuum of course ... but is great to get all the daily dust\/hair quickly and easily . I actually use these attachments on my shop-vac for thorough cleaning .
label: Vacuum

text: These were much cheaper than Miele and seems to work just as well . They look kind of cheap , but I do n't notice any smells when vacuuming so I assume it works well .
label: Vacuum

text: I have 2 children under the age of 3 and this vacuum is my best friend ! I use it virtually every day under the kitchen table and high chairs . I use it to vanquish the crummy dirt that gathers in corners on the floor . I use it occasionally to clean up destroyed Goldfish on the couch and floor . The extendable , flexible hose is super easy to use and so helpful in reaching difficult areas . It could n't be easier to empty . It has lots of good sucking pressure . It 's only limited in 3 ways : the battery life only lasts for about 10 minutes of continual cleaning time , it 's pretty loud and at first freaked out my kids -LRB- but now Big Girl affectionately calls it `` Little Vacuum '' and wants me to get her toes with it -RRB- , and it has a fairly narrow suction diameter . All in all , it 's a bargain at $ 60 and I HIGHLY recommend it for home use with children !

text: I do n't know what I did without it ? Oh yeah , I do ; I had a dirty floor that I tried to clean constantly . I have 3 dogs . text: I 've purchased this vacuum partially due to the raving reviews here . However , my expectations were not fully met . text: We just bought this to replace the same model we 've had for six and a half years . It 's still working , but not with as much gumption , so it 's been retired to the basement . My husband does the vacuuming , and he said `` Get another one just like this '' , so we did .

text: I purchased my Carpet Pro Cpu-lt February , 2011 . Loved it -- not too heavy and it did the job . It quit working in September , 2012 . I 've never owned a vacuum with such a short life , and I only vacuum about once a week . I am really disappointed .

text: I have tried many different vacuums . This is the only one that really gets my house CLEAN , which my allergies certainly appreciate . 1 -RRB- It pulls the fine dust out of the base of my carpet -- now it `` squeeks '' lightly when I walk on it -LRB- previously , the dust lubricated its motion against the pad -RRB- . 2 -RRB- Because it 's bagless , I can empty it right away and the nasty stuff does n't get a chance to fester and smell bad the next time I use the vacuum . I really love using a vacuum that does n't make the air dusty or smelly ! I do make a point of cleaning it out thoroughly -LRB- knocking off the dust outside the house -- I only wash filters occasionally . -RRB- and I wipe off exterior dust with a microfiber `` miracle '' dusting cloth . I do this cleaning after every use so it 's nice , clean , and ready to go for next time . The first time I used it , there was so much dirt in my carpets I needed to empty it after each room ! Usually , though , it 'll do a big chunk of the house before the dust bin is full . I do n't let the dustbin overfill , because then the dust gets pulled deeper into the machine . This is the second Bissell I 've owned . The first one 's rotating brush burned up after two years , which disappointed me . But it 's still worth it for the price !
label: Vacuum

text: I use it constantly several times a day . Love the easiness with the flip thingy on the top . It secures firmly and doesnt releease unless you move the button . I empty it every time and it does n't blow cat hair out of it when it 's on . It 's a huge imporvement from the last thing we used . Easy to mount on the wall and pull\/out on\/off .
label: Vacuum

text: I bought this Bissell vacuum cleaner on the 28th and it 's a relief to know that there are still vacuum cleaners out there that can do the job they 're built to do . With that said , there is one gripe I 'd like to acknowledge . This is n't a major gripe but it was annoying enough to warrant a one star reduction . The assembly of the vacuum handle with one long screw makes the handle of the vacuum cleaner seem a little bit flimsy . What 's more is that the long screw attaches the handle to the body through a deep recess in the screw hole . I feel that this is neither needed nor preferable to a design in which two half length screws on either side of the handle could n't have done a better and more stable job . This I think , is a small oversight and only detracts slightly to the overall quality of the vacuum cleaner . Aside from this , the Bissell works flawlessly is quite capable of picking up all the dirt and debris on my carpets . It 's add-on peripherals add to its utility and help get the job done the way it 's meant to be done . If you 're looking for a decently priced vacuum cleaner with the accessories to match in both looks and utility , look no further because Bissell has both a trusted name and the know how to make a product that works .
label: Vacuum

text: Pros : Good suction , light weight , convenient Cons Short run time -LRB- as advertised -RRB- , clumsy to empty Great for a small apartment
label: Vacuum

text: I am delighted with this vacuum . Almost embarrassed on how much dirt it gets each time . The first use I had to clean the vacuum after only doing two rooms , no dogs or cats , and just recently vacuumed by a Panasonic canister . We also take our shoes off in the house . I 'm am very impressed with the sequential filters they have in this vacuum - at least 5 before the motor and then two on the output including the HEPA filter . While messy to clean , all of the filters and canisters easily come off the vacuum , well designed in this aspect . I find that I need to clean after every use that covers about 1,000 sq. ft of floors . It is very good at picking up yarn - my wife is a knitter and the little slubs were a pain to get with the Panasonic canister I had . Had to scrape away to get - now everything gets taken by this vacuum . The only complaint I have it that the suction around the edges and sides is not great so I have to use the attachment to do the corners and edges near the chairs and couch that are too close to the floor to allow it access . The rotating beater brush almost gives it a self propelling feature . Very good on short carpets , likes to grab the fringes on throw rugs , sort of like trying to take a bone away from a dog . Bottom line , totally happy with it after a few months of use . The noise is even reasonable . Only downside is that wife is too smart to be around when it needs cleaning . YMMV on this aspect ...
label: Vacuum

text: I 've had my vacuum for 3 + months and think it performs well . It easily picked up Christmas tree needles . The one negative is it is heavy to carry upstairs or downstairs . I 'm pleased with my purchase .
label: Vacuum

text: I priced the Eureka Boss Bagged Upright Vacuum Cleaner , 4870SZ here on Amazon and other sites . I decided on the Pet Lover one . I have no problem with refurbished things when done properly , so I opted for the cheaper price . I priced the refurbished ones that included the Pet Paw - ordered the cheapest priced one and waited for delivery . My old vacuum became history about two , nearly three weeks ago . Decided to shop for a decent one instead of buying something last minute at a local store . After getting a bad egg -LRB- First one did n't work , after a good bit of phone time , a second reburbished was mailed to me -RRB- it was just refreshing to get a vacuum that worked . And it works extremely well . Get more dog hair off my floors than any other vacuum . Still not all , small straight chihuahua hair seems to glue itself into the rug , but much more than any other we 've ever owned . And without repeatedly going over the same area at that . Does move the rugs when vacuuming them if not careful . And making sure the switch on the bottom -LRB- Bare floor\/carpet -RRB- after releasing the handle - every time - is quiet annoying . But so far it 's worth the money we paid . Do n't know about the hassle that it took to get it yet though .
label: Vacuum

text: We bought this in 2004 to replace a Hoover upright that constantly ate belts and rollers . We were a bit leary about the glowing claims back then but in the 7 years we have owned it , we have replaced nothing but bags . It picks up as much today as when it was brand new . That 's reliability that you should get when you spend $ 150 + for a vacuum . I 've read the reviews that claim the plastic breaks . We have seen no breakage of anything plastic and it is pretty resilient to the normal banging into furniture . It also has a good long cord to you do n't have to keeping unplugging and moving it to another outlet . The only two negatives , and they are minor , is it is a bit on the heavy side and and there is a flip knob that does n't hold one of the attachments to the unit very well . It has the tendency to fall out . So we just put it away where we know where to find it when we have to vacuum stairs . You ca n't go wrong with this vacuum and for the money it is the best on the market . We 've tried Hooever , Electrolux and even the ultra expensive Oreck .
label: Vacuum

text: best hand vacuum i 've every owned . has power and the pet hair tool actually work . lightweight . have ' t had it long but i like it .
label: Vacuum

text: This vacuum replaces an older Sanyo vacuum that was worn out . We bought the Sanyo from a local vacuum dealer , but we decided not to visit him when we needed a replacement since he was difficult to deal with and charged too much . The Carpet Pro CPU 2T looked a lot like the Sanyo and had good reviews , so I bought it . Upon its arrival , I was amazed at the similarities between the Carpet Pro and the Sanyo . It uses the same bags -LRB- Panasonic Type `` U-3 '' -RRB- and the same belt as well , so we were able to continue using the spare bags & belts from our Sanyo . The CPU 2T does n't have a light , but that 's just as well , since the light on the Sanyo was n't very helpful and tended to burn out frequently . Physically , the CPU 2T looks just like the Sanyo , disassembles like the Sanyo , and sounds just like the Sanyo . Since the Sanyo vacuum was an excellent performer , I was n't surprised by the Carpet Pro 's excellent performance . The accessory port \/ clean-out port on the back of the Carpet Pro is a bit different and requires a bit of attention before vacuuming -LRB- need to be sure it 's not accidentally left open -RRB- , but it 's an improvement on the Sanyo and just part of getting a new vacuum . The CPU 2T is easy to use and is quite effective on carpets & rugs . It 's a powerful vacuum , and it has a long cord that allows me to vacuum several rooms without changing outlets . The hose bracket on the back is an effective handle for carrying the vacuum around , and the weight is low enough to allow my preteen kids to carry the vacuum around the house -LRB- including up and down stairs -RRB- for cleanup . My old Sanyo vacuum served me well for nearly a decade , and I expect the Carpet Pro will do the same . I looked at and tested all kinds of different vacuums before going with the Carpet Pro . Dyson got good reviews , but I felt like I was vacuuming with a large -LRB- expensive -RRB- toy . Many of the others I tried felt flimsy or looked easy to break . Some weighed too much , and some had too many gadgets . I wanted a straightforward , effective , everyday vacuum cleaner for carpets and rugs . The Carpet Pro fit the bill and did n't cost a mint . I 'm happy with it .
label: Vacuum

text: I was so excited to get this product . I thought it was the coolest vacuum ever made , then I opened the box . After waiting for 24 hours for it to charge , I finally got to used it . I once again thought it was the coolest portable vacuum ever made . This lasted for less then 5 minutes . By the time I cleaned the cat hair off of one chair and some cat hair under a couple of tables , it started losing power . IT RAN FOR LESS THAN 5 MINUTES !!! I thought I had a defective product until I began reading the hundreds of reviews that say the same thing . The worst part is that you can not even plug it in and use it . You can not use the unit while it is on the charger ! 24 hours later , you get five more minutes . For a portable vacuum , this thing is expensive . . IT IS NOT WORTH THE MONEY . . YOU WILL BE EXTREMELY DISAPPOINTED . . Mine is getting returned . .
label: Vacuum

text: So far this vac has been great . It makes quick work of pet hair and whatever else the dog drags in the house . It is heavy and a little awkward emptying the dust cup but because it has good suction I do n't care . The battery charge is lasting great so far . I do n't have any complaints yet and hopefully it will be the same in a year .
label: Vacuum

text: I adore the Eureka Boss ! It works like no vacuum I 've ever used . The power of the brush makes it move practically on its own and it picks up dirt , pet hair and all manner of debris with no problem . We have 2 cats , a dog and a 6 year old boy so I 've gotten to test its abilities with sand , small rocks , small sticks - you name it . There are several things to remember when using the vacuum - like remembering to switch from the hose setting to the floor setting , and remembering to lock the brush in the ` on ' position - but once you get used to it there 's no problem . I thought the switch to turn on the brush was really difficult until I realized that it slides right into place when you 've tilted the handle back . And when you put the handle back upright , it disengages the brush so you have to remember to turn it back on again . I also read a review that said this vacuum is loud and heavy but I disagree . It 's about the same weight as my old Dirt Devil and not nearly as loud . I also read that people had trouble tilting the handle back . I did , too , until I realized that the foot button goes to the side , not to the back like other vacuums . I love that all the attachments are secure on the body of the vacuum . I do n't have to worry about knocking them off . On my old machine I eventually just took the attachents off entirely because I got tired of picking them up . My favorite thing about this vacuum is the Pet Paw . Those 2 cats and dog that I mentioned love to sleep on the funiture . It used to take me a lot of time with a lint brush and towel to just get the hair off . But with the Pet Paw the hair is gone in minutes and so is the dust from the furniture .
label: Vacuum

text: I LOVE cordless vacuum cleaners -- very convenient . I like Black & Decker , BUT -- this is the 1952 FIAT \/ RENAULT of cordless vacuum cleaners . Does it work ? Yes , barely & at what trouble . If the designers bothered to give this to their wives for a try-out before production , they 'd all be divorced . Whoever designed this contraption should have his pay firmly fixed to the sales of this sow . Whoever approved this & the tooling costs had better start looking for a new career . Got an enemy ? Give them one for a gift , or better yet , send the vacuum to the Taliban - they 'll surrender after the 1st use ! Positives : 1 . Good charging shoe . 2 . Great battery power -LRB- and noise -RRB- but minimum suction . 3 . Compact folding for storage -LRB- motor homes -RRB- . -- That 's it . Negatives : 1 . VERY Uncomfortable handle - bad design \/ angle . 2 . Weighs a TON because all the weight is 1\/2 way up , including the motor , so it constantly pulls down on your forearm & shoulder instead of being down on the head at the floor where you would barely feel it . 3 . This also causes the head to not have enough weight to hold the brushes in proper contact for maximum performance on any carpet or rug . 4 . This also makes the head hard \/ awkward to control . 5 . Very difficult to glide & has NO tilt \/ steering action - sub-miniature wheels are of NO use on carpet . 6 . Has a crazy back of head ` foot ' extension that gets caught on everything when you pull the vacuum back & forth & flips the head up ! 7 . Emptying the vacuum is mind-boggling , one slight slip trying to hold this monster & it 's all on the floor OR all your filter \/ cover parts are in the garbage . 8 . Returns are NON-EXISTENT . They ca n't sell them so they do n't want them either . THE CURE : HOOVER Platinum LiNX -- The Rolls-Royce ` State-of-the-Art ' of the genre . You 'll fall down & thank God . Costs more BUT you get MORE than your money 's worth , instead of an insult .
label: Vacuum

text: My old vacuum gave out on me and I was forced to find a replacement . I researched vacuums for almost a week . I looked at Consumer Reports and every other online resource that I could find . It was difficult for me to make my choice because the market is flooded with vacuums that have attachments and features out the whazoo . I was leaning towards a more expensive model by another manufacturer . But I recalled that Consumer Reports had named the Bissell Clearview Helix as their number one `` Budget Vacuum . '' As I comtemplated my choice , I reached for the Bissell and walked to the register to make my purchase . When I got home , I immediately assembled the vacuum and gave it a test run . I was amazed at how much dirt it picked up . I 'm a bachelor and do n't have many people over , with the exception of my children and one or two frineds . So my carpet `` APPEARED '' to be clean . Was I surprised . The Bissell picked up dirt that my old vacuum had left behind . So I decided to vacuum my whole apartment . The dirt that the Bissell picked up shocked me even more . I just know that my old vacuum was getting the job done . Boy was I wrong . This vacuum cleaner does n't have a huge price tag , but it delivers big on results . I highly recommend the Clearview Helix . The best part , there 's no bags to purchased . Nothing worst than having to dump those dirty bags .
label: Vacuum

text: This 4870MZ machine cleans very well , but I 'm not the least impressed with the cheap plastic parts . The on\/off switch is cheap and had gone bad causing the wires going to it to melt . It 's a wonder it did n't cause a fire . I would rather spend more money for a quality built machine that will last .

text: I bought a Dyson Ball from HSN , that last close to a month in my power . I owned all kinds of vacuum cleaners , until as last resort I bought this one . Let 's be real , not everything that cost hundreds of dollars have to be good . Tha 's what I tought but , NO. . I paid $ 79.99 and is the best vacuum cleaner ever !! I use it everyday , still working as the first day , is not heavy , has light , I can pick dust from walls and curtains , the sofas !! , it has a long cord , stairs attachment , and many more . The setting for the floor is the best . Hardwood floor and carpets . I love it !!

text: Bought this vacuum cleaner on the strength of other customer reviews . I wanted a cleaner for my second floor so that I would not have to carry my primary vacuum up the stairs as it is so heavy . This one is light in weight , does a really good pick up -LRB- including dog hair -RRB- , has a long cord and because it is slightly narrower goes under more furniture . The only disadvantage I have found thus far is that the inner filter , if rinsed , takes days to dry . Also , the on\/off control is not on the handle . Would I buy it again ? Absolutely .

text: I bought this vacuum a year ago and within a few months the foot pedal release popped off of the machine completely . I had it replaced and it quickly popped off again . This time the clear plastic housing broke and would cost $ 29 to replace the part and $ 29 in labor . It can only be replaced with the same cheap clear plastic housing . It still works , and though heavy , i have been ok with it 's performance . However , without the foot pedal , it will not stay locked in the upright position and falls when nudged or tugged slightly . This makes some operations difficult , especially when using the hose attachement .
label: Vacuum

text: When I first got this little vacuum I loved it . Now , just over a year later I threw it away . It used to work really well for wood floors and above floor cleaning . The suction was ok , but not good on carpet of any kind . The attachments are easy to use and change . I bought it after having back surgery because was not able to push and pull an upright and hate dragging around a canister . This comes with a strap that you can put over your shoulder and it has a very long cord , so it 's very convenient . The only problem is that after only a year it is completely dead . If you are looking to buy a new vacuum every year this one should do the trick .

text: I bought this vacuum yesterday at Target . My old oreck was not doing the job and I 'm sorry I ever bought that thing in the first place . But , this Bissell really did do the job . I was dumbfounded when I saw all the hair and dust in the container . I have a cat and a dog and I thought I was keeping up with the hair . Was I ever wrong ! I feel so much better knowing how much cleaner my house is . The little turbobrush thingy worked great on the upholstery . I did the upholstery and the pet beds with it . For the price I do n't think you can go wrong with this machine . I only gave it 4 stars because I 've only had it a day and I thought the hose could be a little longer . Even so , I 'm thinking of getting another one for the downstairs . I recommend buying this machine .
label: Vacuum

text: I have just a few small rugs and some steps and I love this little vacuum cleaner . It is quite powerful and easy to use . Its not that much fun to clean out the filter but if you do it outside its no big deal . Easy to store . I recommend it for small jobs . Oh , and I have a long haired cat who sheds as much as 5 cats and this works really well !

text: This vacuum has given us nothing but problems since we have had it . After the first month of using it the suction of the hose would not work properly if the whole sweeper was not taken apart and cleaned in the sink after only a week worth of uses . Then it would start to stink like rotting fish after using and washing under warm water , which the instructions say to do to clean the filters . Would never buy this or recommend to anyone !
label: Vacuum

text: I 've had a hand-held version of this vacuum for 2 years before it broke . After that I went for a couple of years with the stick vacuums and had quite a few of them - nothing came close to the performance of my hand-held B&D . They simply do n't have enough power \/ suction to be effective . Then , I saw my B&D in the stick form . After having it for a couple of weeks at this point I 'm glad to see it 's every bit as good as my old one . This is the best stick cordless vacuum I 've ever owned and I owned a few . The only con this vacuum has is noise - not the quietest vacuum out there but I 'll trade the noise for power .
label: Vacuum

text: I 've tested this unit , and the Dyson handheld , and though the Dyson was still more powerful , the Flex Vac came in a very close second . And you just ca n't beat the price ! -LRB- $ 55-75 for a Flex Vac vs $ 175-300 for a Dyson ! -RRB- Unlike many handhelds I 've owned , this unit does a great job on kitty litter ! It picks it up , and does n't spit it back out . Many people are complaining about the battery life . I can tell you that the Dyson lasts about as long . Personally , I only use this for small jobs , and have yet to run out of power before I was done . The filter does fill rather quickly , and I suggest emptying the unit after each use . \*\* Open the vacuum directly over a trash can , or you may end up with another mess to clean up . \*\* The pet attachment works well for cleaning long cat hair off fabric . It does not work as well on dog hair , or short cat hair . It will pick it up , if it 's in clumps , but it 's not great at getting anything that has woven itself into the fabric . I do wish Black & Decker would sell a wand and floor attachment for this vac . I 've been using the wand and hard floor attachment from my Kirby upright w\/it , and although it 's not a perfect solution , it does seem to work . Now I can vacuum up the kitty litter on my laundry room floor ! I 've added some pictures of this to the product page . Picks up : Kitty litter , fine dust & sand , sawdust , pet hair , etc. . I also picked up some nuts , bolts & wood chips with this unit . It was an accident , & I would n't actually recommend using anything but a shop vac for that , but it did pick them up . Pros Powerful & effective Works on kitty litter Cheaper than a Dyson Comes with attachments , caddy & has a hose Looks nice & is always handy Other vacuum attachments seem to -LRB- mostly -RRB- fit -LRB- unlike the Dyson handheld -RRB- Cons No option to buy a wand and\/or floor attachment No wall mount Battery life is a bit short Slightly less powerful than the Dyson
label: Vacuum

text: OK Vacuum with pros and cons . First the Pros . I like the fact that when the vacuum has it 's handle locked in the upright position it stops the brush . Also the feature of switching the suction from the floor to attachments is nice . The vacuum rolls nicely but is a little heavier than some other like vacuums on the market . The biggest con I found is the design of the brush . I 'm finding that I 'm replacing the brush after four years of usable because the bearing in the brush will seizes . The belt is Kevlar and not made out of rubber . The Kevlar is nice because it will last longer but when the brush locks up because the bearing go out it will start smoking and will start the wood brush soldering .
label: Vacuum

text: This is my second `` Boss '' vacuum and I highly reccommend this model ! It cleans very well and has great suction ! The onboard tools are very handy and it is quick to switch from floor to hose ! The cord is long enough to reach many areas without having to unplug it ! text: Well , apparently my walkman is now obsolete . No one sent me the memo . After a lot of research , talking to dealers and friends purchased this MP3 player . Alot of in depth reviews on this out there , I 'll give you the highlights of mine . Pros-very versatile , robust with a ton of memory , well built and actually more attractive than the other players on the market . I-Tunes does not require membership -LRB- yet -RRB- and allows you to transfer digital media -LRB- MP3 , CD and video -RRB- and will recognize most discs you put in . Otherwise you 'll hafta manually type album , name , artist etc. . The unit itself comes with a large -LRB- for an MP3 player -RRB- screen and built-in games . I-Tunes must be aquired from the official site or transfered from another computer . I-Tunes allows you to drag and drop songs from your list to the iPod without havig to retain them on your computer , saving computer space . If you need to restore the player due to errors however , you 'll have to reload those selections to your computer . Cons - Has a mediocre Equalizer and output is far less robust than a portable CD or Cassette player . Most use the factory ear buds and are quite content . I prefer wide range ear cannons though . Needs a glass face , not plastic with poor scratch resistance . Hard disks on these units can seize and are indeed fragile . Refurbished units are available with `` debugged '' hard disks online . Battery life is very exagerrated , under normal use you 'll get half of the advertised time between charges . The box data is under really , really ideal situations . When the battery wears out , you 'll be stuck getting a whole new iPod due to lack of factory support or really high replacement fees , if not under warranty . Comes with cloth pouch , ear buds and USB 2.0 compatible cord . Mass market has many low cost peripherals , much more than are available for it 's competitors . So , ho-hum , if you 're forced to portable digital this is the one . My iPod is full of vinyl record , Stereo-8 and Open Reel transfers , thanks to my Sony CDR . After 4 months with it , it 's still the best one . Recommended .

We returned it to Amazon -LRB- great service by them -RRB- and had a second unit in a couple of days but it had the SAME PROBLEMS . By this point I felt that I should go through Creative for help - not Amazon 's fault , why should they continue to pay for shipping etc. . Here is my warning - terrible customer\/tech service . To start , their website , when it worked , was not user friendly or helpful . Secondly , you ca n't find a number to call when you click the ` contact us ' button . Finally , even if you click the ` send an email ' button , you get sent around in circles to ` list your problem ' and never get to send an email for help . So , I opened the literature in the box , found a number , had to pay for the call and I ended up with a tech guy in Singapore . Though he was very nice - he did not solve or even seem to understand the problems . As my 60 works just fine through my computer , my USB ports are not an issue , yet he continued to tell to disconnect them and reconnect them . Then he told me to do the reset - great , but one itty bitty problem - to do the reset the battery has to be charged ... it charges through the USB port ... which it wo n't recognize and therefore wo n't charge . Hmmm ... Finally , he said I should update the firmware ... once again that pesky little issue with NO USB CONNECTIVITY . So , they did not solve my issue . His suggestion , send it back . OK , do you want my address for the call tag ? No , you -LRB- the customer -RRB- have to pay to ship it . WHAT ? I requested that someone from customer service in the US call me . I got a call from Singapore by a friendly lady who informed to try all the same things I had already tried . The really frustating part was that she was even reading my case file from the previous call and KNEW that those things did not work . She then told me that they would be happy to take back the Zen and run a diagnostic on it and either replace it or fix it and ship it back , BUT I had to pay the freight both ways . I hit the roof - and have requested a manager to call me on Monday . I 'll let you know if that even happens and what transpires if it does . So , as my wife is really wanting her MP3 player , I did another return on Amazon tonight . Only to get an email that they will now NOT send me a third unit -LRB- ca n't blame them really -RRB- because there is possibly a widespread problem with these units . They are telling me to wait a couple of weeks and order again . Oh , how happy this makes my wife who was so excited about it FOUR WEEKS AGO when I ordered AND still does n't have an MP3 player that works . SO , suggestion - wait for a while or save yourself the potential frustration and buy something else . Maybe their help and customer concern will get the message . I want to return my 60 gig to Creative and have them buy all the accessories we purchased . I want to get all my money back and go iPod at this point . Which is sad because my not being a fan of the proprietary nature of iTunes etc. was the whole reason I chose the Zen in the first place . But at this point it seems the lesser of two evils because the help and customer service at Creative has me boiling . Good luck . UPDATE : MAY 1 , 2007 : Creative did have a manager call . He was polite , but not helpful in resolving the problem . In fact , they deny that there is a widespread problem , it must be my bad luck to receive two units with 100 % dead batteries . So , my wife gave up and purchased a 30 gig iPod today . She seems happy .

text: First to the folks with hard drive errors -LRB- or that wanna bigger HD -RRB- ... I do n't think the HD itself has failed . I sent mine in , had it replaced , got it back , it worked for awhile , and then I started having problems with it again . I really think the problem is that there is no way to do a ` low level ' format on the HD - so , if you get a hiccup on it , well , you 're toast . I think some of the older firmware versions -LRB- it 's now up to 3.0 -RRB- had problems and\/or if you do n't run it on AC as well as a direct 2.0 USB connection it can introduce this issue . Now , how do you fix it ? I fixed mine -LRB- I think -RRB- by opening the case -LRB- carefully remove the 4 small screws and it 'll clamshell open -RRB- , then you see the HD . That noise you hear is your warranty voiding , if you still had one ... Carefully disconnect the HD -LRB- DO N'T PULL THAT RIBBON CABLE ! -RRB- I used a screwedriver to pry the connector out . Now , if the HD is truly dead , you can buy a new -LRB- say 100Gb -RRB- 2.5 '' notebook drive , and just plug it back in . Or , you can attach the HD to your computer -LRB- I used a USB to ATA adapter -RRB- , and then use the OS to low level format it -LRB- just do n't select ` quick format ' -RRB- . This process will also tell you if it 's bad ... After it finishes , put it back into the unit , power up\/reformat it , and off you go . Second , on the battery issue , it 's 18 + hours on music . Plus , I bought an external battery pack I can run it off of -LRB- 6V , from Socket -RRB- . It works quite fine , watched movies for 4 hours + never ran out ... Finally , for the folks who complained about losing data . IT 'S A HARD DRIVE ! Hard drives fail . This has nothing to do with this technology ! Make a backup -LRB- all my stuff goes onto my computer , and THEN onto the unit , so I have 2 copies -RRB- . text:
I loved my TiVo from the moment I installed it -LRB- which takes a little while , but then it 's over -RRB- . However , I have had three units fail because of the extensively discussed TiVo modem issue . If you have not had a hardware problem yet , then you are fortunate .

Please do n't take my word for it -- evaluate the hardware issues for yourself by browsing the excellent TiVo forum ... prior to purchasing a unit , for very helpful advice . Read the FAQs and try searching for `` dead modem . ''

Apparently the modem is highly sensitive to any power fluctuation in the telephone line , and is very easily burned out . Because the modem is built as part of the TiVo chip , it can not simply be replaced with a better modem .

If you decide to purchase a TiVo unit , be sure to buy from a reliable merchant -LRB- reports about Amazon are good -RRB- and also purchase an iron-clad extended warranty . Otherwise , after 90 days , all Phillips TiVo repairs are $ 99 .

Sorry to be so negative about the hardware , but I will repeat that the TiVo service itself is terrific !

text:
I have owned a ReplayTV 3060 since they began shipping . If you 've never owned a DVR , you owe it to yourself to try one . It will change your life . Prime time is whenever you sit down in front of the tube .

The ReplayTV 5160 is a worthy upgrade . Every aspect and feature of this product show careful thought , planning , and design . It 's attractive , quieter than the 3060 was , and much more capacious .

I 've read many reviews in which geeks have promoted a `` build-it-yourself '' solution . As a geek who \* has \* built his own , I can say with assurance that we 're just not there yet . MythTV is a marvellous programming effort , but it does n't begin to approach the ease of use and `` set and forget '' qualities of ReplayTV .

The folks at ReplayTV are gifted programmers and talented designers . The Replay 5160 is the pinnacle of their creation , and contains features that caused the parent company to be sued out of existence . The new parent will continue to design and market ReplayTV , but without the offending features . This is the one to buy !

text:
I 've had my Series 2 TiVo 40-hour unit for about one year now and have fully converted to this revolutionary way of watching television . I would never consider myself to be a technology geek to any degree but having TiVo is a true blessing since it was relatively easy to install and is a snap to operate . I 've experienced zero problems with my unit and have no complaints ; in one year all I 've had to do is replace the remote batteries twice .

Looking back , the transition to TiVo was an experience in itself . I admit it was difficult to get used to the concept of not relying on the clock to watch my favorite shows but rather simply recording a show and watching it when it 's convenient for \* me \* , not the networks . Zipping through the commercials is a breeze once I got accustomed to the 30-second skip feature ; it 's great how TiVo automatically synchronizes to the beginning of the show so you never miss anything . With the help of season passes and the `` To Do '' list I can visit family and friends without worrying about what I 'm missing on television .

The total affect of TiVo becomes greatly apparent when watching television at a friends house who does n't have TiVo . The urge to fast-forward , pause and rewind jolts me when I realize that I 'm just watching `` normal '' television . But never fear , returning home I am always greatly comforted by the TiVo features that I 've grown to love .

During the past year I 've been successful in recruiting several friends to TiVo and they have had minimal complaints . The only issue worth mentioning is NBC 's recent tendency to begin and end shows at odd times and making last minute schedule changes often resulting in cutting off episodes if TiVo is not enlightened of these events . TiVo tries to catch these instances and sends out important updates but it continues to be an issue . This was a particular concern during the series finale of Friends last month .

In all honesty TiVo is very economical if you factor in how many hours of enjoyment you 'll get out of that black box each month , so there 's really no excuse to refuse to join the TiVolution movement ! Get TiVo today and you 'll never look back .
label: Video Recorder

text: This machine does what I was hoping it would do : make excellent copies of HD cable source movies . It will make BluRay discs or standard DVDS from single or double layer blank DVD discs . I have also not been able to successfully use the timer that is built into the software , so I need to be present when the recording of the movie is finished to turn it off . I am using the software that came with the device . text: I bought the Hauppauge 1445 HD-PVR Gaming Edition to record video from my XBox 360 . It works great , but after a day of struggling with the device and being ready to rip my hair out , hopefully I can give you some details and save you from similar frustration . The box comes with everything you need to get started : the Hauppauge itself , an AC adapter , a component cable capable of connecting any of the current-generation gaming systems -LRB- PS3\/360\/Wii -RRB- , a male-to-male component cable for passthrough to your TV , a USB cable for connecting to your computer , and a software disc . Keep in mind that you 'll be doing all the capture on a computer ; this device does not have an integrated hard drive , or the ability to add one . I specifically bought the gaming edition so I could get the included multi-device component cable , because aside from my Wii , I hook everything up via HDMI . If you 're buying the gaming edition for the green light on the device ... go ahead . If the color of the light does n't matter to you , DO N'T buy the gaming edition . I made the mistake of believing that for five extra dollars , I was going to get a high-quality cable , despite being an analog signal split between three possible input connections . After plugging the cables , I realized my mistake . There was ghosting , what some might call the `` halo effect '' , around everything . It was most pronounced around text . This effect appeared at 720p and 1080i resolutions , and also appeared in recordings , proving that the problem was with the cable itself and not with my setup . 480i and 480p had no ghosting issue , so if you 're planning on recording Wii only , you can make do with the included cable . I had to go out and buy a dedicated cable for my XBox 360 . I 'd recommend you do the same , and stick with the cheaper `` blue '' model . After that frustration , the biggest problem was getting the damn thing to actually work . I connected all the cables and the USB cable as instructed , popped in the disc , and installed the device drivers and the ArcSoft ShowBiz software . I launched the ShowBiz capture module , and ... no video ! I had all the correct inputs selected -LRB- Hauppauge device , component input , rear stereo audio -RRB- , and the preview window showed nothing . Trying to capture video despite that yielded an error when stopping the capture . I must have changed literally every device and format setting , trying to find whatever quirk in my settings was causing the issue . I even installed the drivers and software on another computer to see if it was my laptop 's fault . As a last resort , I visited the Hauppauge website and downloaded the drivers from the Support page . After updating my drivers ... lo and behold , the preview video was there ! Why the included drivers did n't work from the get-go , I have no idea . What 's even more confusing is that it was n't included as a suggestion in any of the troubleshooting documentation I could find , nor did the driver changelog suggest that it was solving any problem in my setup . So , I spent the better half of my day just getting the capture set up and getting quality video going . I 'm happy to say that following those issues , the device works flawlessly . Once you start capture up , the device lights up bright to let you know that it 's busy at work , encoding the video and transferring it to your computer according to whatever settings you have set . There 's no lag on the TV itself , despite the Hauppauge being in-between . The video as well as the audio will be played back on the computer with a bit of a delay though , but you can feel free to mute your system while recording happens to avoid the distracting audio . You do n't really need a powerful system to capture video , because all the encoding is done on the Hauppauge itself -LRB- editing is a different story -RRB- . You have a large range of mbps\/sec qualities to choose from , whether you want to bitrate to be constant or variable , and whether you want stereo sound to be encoded as AAC or AC3 . In addition , you have a range of output formats to choose from , including . TS , . M2TS , and . MP4 . These are organized according to whichever device you plan to use to play the media back -LRB- AVCHD , PS3 , and XBox 360 , respectively -RRB- . I recommend recording in . TS format , regardless of what device you 'll use . . MP4 has audio sync issues , so avoid using that format . I cranked mine up to the highest variable bitrate and AC3-encoded audio at 720p in . TS format , and it cost me about 1.6 GB for a 20 minute video . Not too shabby ! I have n't compared various bitrates and their effect on file size , but you can probably make your space go a lot farther with a bit of experimentation . The quality was great ; what I saw on my TV was what recorded , with no audio hiccups or stuttering of any kind . If you 're planning on doing some trimming of your videos , and maybe adding some transitions , basic effects , or text , then ArcSoft ShowBiz will do the trick for you , complete with the ability to upload directly to YouTube . For me , I needed something with some more options -LRB- no fade through black , ArcSoft ? Really ? -RRB- . I used AVS Video Editor , which has a much more robust set of options , and best of all , takes your . TS format video without a fuss . Be warned that it will include a watermark upon publishing if you do n't buy the full version . Unfortunately , you likely wo n't be able to `` cut out the middleman '' , so to speak , when capturing video . AVS could n't capture video from the Hauppauge , so I 'm stuck with capturing the video with ArcSoft ShowBiz and opening up the video in AVS . I ca n't say for sure if any other Windows-based video capture software will work , but I had no such luck . Those of you using a Mac or Linux , there are third-party solutions out there to get you rolling . I have n't tested them yet , so I ca n't tell you what type of experience you 'll have . Overall , the Hauppauge HD-PVR does a great job of what it 's supposed to do : Capture high quality video from your game console or other component video device and provide an acceptable set of editing and uploading options . I took off one star for the initial frustration and the poor-quality gaming cable . Once it 's working , it does a great job of what it promises to do . Happy recording ! If you have questions , feel free to leave a comment on the review .
label: Video Recorder

text: I 'm not going to sing the praises of TiVo , as many others have done so much more eloquently than I can . Suffice it to say , all the good stuff is true , and most of the bad stuff is blown out of proportion . Instead , I 'll share some tips for squeezing every last ounce of enjoyment out of your TiVo : - If you have broadband or DSL , get your TiVo hooked up to your home network . You no longer need a phone line for service updates , you can transfer recorded programs to a PC -LRB- with the free TiVo Desktop software -RRB- , and you can access digital music and photos on your computer from your TiVo . For people who are n't quite ready to shell out for a media center PC , this is a cheap and more than adequate first step . - As mentioned in the featured review , there are several ways to upgrade your TiVo hard drive without shelling out hundreds of dollars for a new receiver . -LRB- ... -RRB- . \*\*\* PLEASE NOTE : Opening your receiver will void your warranty , and you need to be comfortable handling internal PC components to perform the upgrade . \*\*\* That being said , it 's an easy enough process and I 've had no problems with my unit since the `` operation '' . Some Google searching will lead you to the directions for this process - try `` hinsdale tivo upgrade '' . - Be diligent about rating programs , but be careful as well . The more you rate , the more on-point the suggestions are . Just be careful about being too enthusiatic in applying the thumbs-up . I made the mistake of giving two thumbs up to Law & Order , only to come back from a long weekend and find over a dozen episodes waiting for me . That 's a little too much courtroom drama for my tastes . - Do n't let the lack of a handy phone line stop you from joining the TiVo revolution . There is a wireless phone adapter that will work with TiVo , and you can also use a wireless network adapter to connect to your home network -LRB- this is what I do -RRB- . If you decide to go for the wireless netwrok , be sure to visit the TiVo site to see which adapter works with your particular model . One thing I can say for sure , you wo n't regret your purchase !

text: \*\*\* UPDATE 5\/4\/08 : After over 1 year of use , I 've made a few updates to the review . Overall , still very happy ! Just wanted to add some more useful information and minor updates . \*\*\* I 'm currently using 2 QSPDVR04 PCI cards along with 8 various Q-See camears . The maximum is 4 PCI cards -LRB- 16 channels -RRB- per system . The box includes the software , users guide , pci card , and 4 BNC - -RRB- RCA adapters -LRB- the 4 connectors on the card itself are BNC -LRB- video only -RRB- . The RCA adapters are only used for the low-end camears - which do n't normally work that well anyways . Also note that the Q-See hardware and software both appears to be identical to the Swann gear . I do suggest a much better system than their minimum requirements , especially if you are going to use more than 1 PCI card . In my case , the 2 cards are installed in a Dell Optiplex GX280 , P4 2.4 Ghz , 2GB of RAM , and 250GB HD . It 's a relatively cheap system and has been dedicated as a DVR PC for the cameras . I originally had a slower PIII 1GHz system doing the job , but when I added a 2nd PCI card , that was a little too much for the old Dell . While this is still well above the min sys requirements , it 's still a good idea to make use of the `` scheduled reboot '' feature if you notice things getting sluggish after awhile . This is possibly due to some bugs in the s\/w and may be fixed in the future . I always install the latest software from the website - very important . I 'm using the remote web playback\/recording software so 4 other computers in the house can monitor the cameras in realtime as well as PC 's located outside of the house , via the Internet -LRB- may need to make changes to your router -RRB- . I should also inform you that some of the `` advanced '' features may require a bit of time to figure out for novice PC users . Especially considering the manual looks as though it was written in another language and then poorly translated into English . I have a few cameras connected to these cards . -LRB- 1 -RRB- QSVC456C camera , -LRB- 3 -RRB- QS2350C cameras , -LRB- 2 -RRB- QOCDC cameras , and -LRB- 2 -RRB- QPSCDCA indoor only cameras . The DVR software is setup to record 24\/7 as well as make note of motion detection . This allows me to either playback everything , or just video where motion was detected . But thanks to a 250GB HD , it can record for months without running out of space . The card is only able to capture 7 FPS for each camera -LRB- 30fps total -RRB- . This also applies to watching video in realtime . So images will appear `` choppy , '' which is expected for something in this price range . It 's honestly not a big issue though . The recorded image quality is fairly good , at a maximum of 640x480 resolution it works well for home surveillance . Just do n't expect to zoom into a licence plate 100 ft away like you see on TV . : -RRB- The software also allows you choose several viewing options , such as auto scroll mode that goes through each camera , in full screen , one at a time . Or , display them all at once on the screen in various formats . You have the ability to set recording to schedule , manual , and\/or motion detection -LRB- the sensitivity is adjustable -RRB- . The playback software works very well . Just select a date , then scroll through the time to pick the spot you want to watch . You can watch 1 camera at a time , all of them at once , or pick the ones you want . It 's similar to other video editing software that you have likely seen on your PC . The only negative here is that if you want to watch it play back in realtime , the the fastest it will go is 4x . The use of ` motion recording ' is recommended due to this over sight . I 'm hoping a software update will fix this important `` feature . '' Pros : \* Very inexpensive DVR PCI Card \* Easy installation \* Basic but very functional software \* Expandable up to 4 PCI cards \* Good video playback\/record quality . \* Remote software for Live\/Recorded playback via LAN\/WAN . Cons : \* Advanced features may confuse some users \* Manual needs to be re-writting in `` English '' \* Fast Forward needs to be drastically increased \* 30fps shared between 4 cameras Bottom line : while there are better dedicated DVR 's and PCI DVR 's out there - considering the price and functionality of the QSPDVR04 , I rate this card at 5 stars , and consider it highly recommended for and home\/small business surveillance . Even though I did n't have any issues with the card or software , keep in mind that I am a very technical user . If you do n't feel comfortable opening your PC or are a novice user - be sure to read the users guide on the Q-See site before purchase to make sure you know what you 're getting into . : -RRB-

text: I 've had this recorder for about 10 days and have used it for both voice recordings , playback of voice and playback of music files and an audiobook . I 've also used the USB recharging feature , copied audiobook & music files to the `` Music '' folder from my Dell laptop USB port running Vista Home Premium -LRB- 64 bit -RRB- . The unit is small , and easily fits in a pocket . Overall , I 've found the WS-500M -LRB- silver -RRB- recorder to be excellent ! I 'm very pleased with it . Reason I bought it : I 'm a self-employed writer and wanted to record story ideas , meetings , interviews , etc. . Plus for other meetings where I wanted a record of what was said , saving me from writing everything down . I had been using an HP iPAQ PDA -LRB- hx2495b -RRB- Pocket PC with built-in voice recorder . But over the years I 've had trouble with Windows Mobile 5 hanging before going into a meeting -LRB- slow , doggy OS -RRB- . When it worked it was fine , but then it recorded to main memory and when that ran out , the recording stopped of course . So I bought the right tool for the job . Other reviews : I read the reviews on here & elsewhere before buying it and it is highly rated . One person mentioned the 30 page manual as being long , but that includes the instructions in four languages -LRB- i.e. EN , FR , ES , PT -RRB- with the English -LRB- EN -RRB- section being 11 pages . There is a foldout section with diagrams the instructions refer to . Another compliant I 've seen is the hard to use menu , I disagree with that , the menus are easy to use . I have computer background though , so electronic devices come easy to me . I do have some experience with using the Olympus WS-210S -LRB- 256 MB -RRB- recorder , and this unit is nicer . When I first used the WS-210S the menu took some getting used to , but I think the WS-500M has improved the menu . The WS-210S is used at our church , where it is plugged into the sound system 's mixer -LRB- via patch cable to the MIC jack on the unit -RRB- and we record the priest 's homily . Our priest has a wireless microphone . We then convert the WMA format to MP3 -LRB- free utility : `` Efficient WMA MP3 Converter '' -RRB- , edit out unwanted audio -LRB- start\/end -RRB- with the free `` Audacity '' software & export as MP3 , then upload to our parish website as a podcast . The WS-210S only records in WMA format , while the WS-500M can record in WMA or MP3 \* . -LRB- \* You need to download the free firmware upgrade from the Olympus site to record in MP3 . -RRB- Nice added feature ! The backlight feature is nice . The USB recharging feature allows you to recharge the included AAA NiMH rechargeable battery by plugging into a USB port on your computer . Pressing <STOP> & plugging into the USB port only allows charging , so you ca n't transfer files & recharge at the same time . I do n't see this as a drawback . The recharging feature was important to me . Alkalines are costly & end up in the landfill , unless you can recycle them . There is a switch that allows you to use it in either Voice or Music modes . `` Voice '' allows you to select the five different folders -LRB- A-E -RRB- , record\/playback audio , etc. . `` Music '' mode allows access to the `` Music '' folder -LRB- where you can copy music or other audio files -LRB- i.e. audiobooks -RRB- to it from your computer -RRB- . You can copy whole folders into the `` Music '' folder from your computer to group music , audiobooks separately . There are no playlists , so you would have to name the tracks in a sequential format to play in that order . If you want an MP3 player , you should look for that first . The primary purpose of this unit is a voice recorder , so the added feature of playing MP3\/WMA 's is nice , but it lacks features -LRB- i.e. playlists -RRB- found in MP3 players . The sound through the speaker is adequate , but as others have noted , it sounds much better -LRB- CD quality -RRB- through the included ear-buds -LRB- or your own pair -RRB- . You can also plug external speakers into the headphone jack , or with a patch cable into your car stereo -LRB- if it has an input AUX type jack -RRB- . If you set the voice recording to the lowest quality though , I do n't know how that will sound . So if your recording live music somewhere , set the recording quality in the menu to the highest . Someone said the recorder will pickup table noise when placed on a table for a meeting . I would suggest placing the recorder on a book or stack of papers . Someone said there is no ON\/OFF switch , actually there is and it 's the `` HOLD '' switch . It 's oddly named , but in the HOLD position it 's OFF , while sliding it the other way is ON . It has a nice feature of turning off after a couple minutes of non-use -LRB- i.e. not recording , not playing back or pressing buttons -RRB- . There is a light on the front & back that shows when it 's recording or charging . When recharging is done , the light turns off . You can get more tech . spec 's at the Olympus site , do n't buy it there as it 's list price -LRB- $ 100 -RRB- .

That means I ca n't plug it in and run it from my laptop . I have to depend on the batteries , which , as I said , do n't last long .
